items.title: U.S. FDA Approves Lupin's Ranluspec (ranibizumab-hkdz) as Interchangeable Biosimilar to Lucentis
items.summary: Lupin Limited has received U.S. FDA approval for Ranluspec™ (ranibizumab-hkdz), an interchangeable biosimilar to Lucentis® (ranibizumab) for the treatment of wet age-related macular degeneration (AMD), diabetic macular edema (DME), and other serious retinal diseases. It is the first interchangeable biosimilar ranibizumab available in both vials and pre-filled syringes (PFS) in the United States.
items.compliance_impact: This approval expands interchangeable biosimilar options in ophthalmology. Health systems and pharmacy networks can substitute Ranluspec for Lucentis without requiring provider intervention, subject to state laws.
items.key_actions:
1. Update pharmacy formularies to include Ranluspec as a cost-effective alternative to Lucentis.
2. Review state-specific laws regarding interchangeable biosimilar substitution.
3. Educate staff on the availability of both vial and pre-filled syringe (PFS) options.
